ASD: spacecraft telemetry data with one second sample rate.
CalIt2: sensor data collected from various environments with one second sample rate.
CICIDS: network traffic data with a one-second sample rate.
Creditcard: credit card transactions with one second sample rate.
Daphnet: accelerometer data from Parkinson's disease patients with a 50 Hz sample rate.
Exathlon: sensor data from wearable devices with one second sample rate..
GECCO: traffic flow data from various urban locations with five minutes sample rate.
Genesis: sensor data from an industrial plant with one minute sample rate.
MSL: sensor data from a manufacturing plant with one minute sample rate.
NYC: traffic flow data from New York City with one minute sample rate.
PSM: power system measurements from electrical grids with one second sample rate.
PUMP: pump performance data with one minute sample rate.
SKAB: sensor data from a smart building with one minute sample rate.
SMAP: soil moisture data from a satellite with one day or two days or three days sample rate.
SMD: sensor data from industrial machines with one second sample rate.
SWAN: sensor data from a water quality monitoring system with one hour sample rate.
SWAT: streamflow data from various watersheds with one day sample rate.
GHL: contains the status of 3 reservoirs such as the temperature and level. Anomalies indicate changes in max temperature or pump frequency.
MITDB: contains 48 half-hour excerpts of two-channel ambulatory ECG recordings, obtained from 47 subjects studied by the BIH Arrhythmia Laboratory between 1975 and 1979.
SVDB: includes 78 half-hour ECG recordings chosen to supplement the examples of supraventricular arrhythmias in the MIT-BIH Arrhythmia Database.
LTDB: is a collection of 7 long-duration ECG recordings (14 to 22 hours each), with manually reviewed beat annotations.
CATSv2: consists of commands, external stimuli, and telemetry readings of a simulated complex dynamical system with 200 injected anomalies.
TAO: contains 575,648 records with 3 attributes which are collected from the Tropical Atmosphere Ocean project.
OPPORTUNITY: which comprises the readings of motion sensors recorded while users executed typical daily activities.
GutenTAG: every time series consists of one or more base oscillations (BOs), optional trends, and optional random white noise.
DLR: The DLR dataset focuses on human activity recognition, containing sensor measurements from wearable devices that track 12 different activities.
ECG: The ECG dataset comprises features extracted from electrocardiogram signals, where abnormal heartbeat patterns are marked as outliers, serving crucial medical diagnostic purposes.
KDD-99: The HTTP dataset encompasses network traffic data with varying trends, noise, and seasonality patterns.
